summary: Soundboard recording of a solid show with standout Sugaree, Brown Eyed Women and Terrapin Station; occasional muddiness and track‑label quirks.
review: The performance opens explosively with a high‑energy Not Fade Away → Sugaree → Wang Dang Doodle run and delivers several highlights such as a superb Brown Eyed Women, an extended Terrapin Station, and a biting Bucket that many fans rate above Throwing Stones.  The soundboard source is generally clear and “great,” though a few sections (especially the rhythm section on Brent’s organ) are described as muddy and the track labeling is confused.  Overall the show is enjoyable with a few mid‑set lulls (Jack A Roe, Tennessee) that keep the rating in the mixed‑positive range.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is strong with many praised songs and good band interplay (≈4/5), while recording quality is solid but not flawless due to occasional muddiness (≈3.5/5). Weighted average yields ~3.9.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and well‑mixed; reviewers call it a “great recording,” but note some mud in the rhythm section when Brent is on organ and occasional track‑label confusion.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A thunderous opening set that loses a bit of momentum after Jack A Roe, but the second set recovers with an expansive Terrapin Station and strong jams, ending on a high note.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s vocals are highlighted (nice backing, lyric slip noted) and his guitar tone is praised; occasional vocal ‘malfunction’ mentioned but harmless.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass work gets praise (“Good to hear Phil”) though the mix can be muddy when the rhythm section swells; he also extends Terrapin Station and appears in the Scarlet jam.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (organ/keys) is “up in the mix,” sings with Garcia, and adds a distinctive organ texture that some describe as slightly muddy.
